nodes.description: Linus Torvalds' Unix-like kernel, paired with GNU userland and a community development model, ran much of the internet within a decade. Validated open-source as a serious mode of software production.

nodes.label: Lithium-ion battery
nodes.description: Sony commercialized the Yoshino-Goodenough lithium-ion cell. Made portable electronics ubiquitous and electric vehicles and grid storage feasible.

nodes.label: Snowball Earth hypothesis
nodes.description: Joe Kirschvink proposed that the Earth was repeatedly fully glaciated in the late Proterozoic. A radical, then increasingly supported reading of deep-time climate.

nodes.label: Shor's quantum factoring algorithm
nodes.description: Peter Shor showed a quantum computer could factor integers in polynomial time, threatening RSA encryption. Crystallized the practical motivation for building quantum machines.

nodes.label: GPS becomes fully operational
nodes.description: The US Global Positioning System reached full constellation, with civilian access opened in 2000. Enabled an entire location-aware economy and required general-relativistic timing corrections.

nodes.label: Dolly the cloned sheep
nodes.description: Ian Wilmut's team produced a viable mammal from a somatic cell nucleus. Demonstrated that differentiated cells could be reprogrammed to totipotency.

nodes.label: Diamond's Guns, Germs, and Steel
nodes.description: Jared Diamond argued that environmental endowments largely shaped the differential trajectories of human societies. A widely read - and widely contested - synthesis of geography, ecology, and history.

nodes.label: PageRank and modern search
nodes.description: Page and Brin treated the Web's hyperlink graph as votes of authority. Underpinned Google's takeover of search and recast information retrieval around link structure.

nodes.label: The Anthropocene proposed
nodes.description: Paul Crutzen and Eugene Stoermer named a proposed new geological epoch in which human activity is the dominant agent of change. Reframed environmental science and policy.

nodes.label: Putnam's Bowling Alone
nodes.description: Robert Putnam quantified four decades of declining civic association in the United States and theorized 'social capital.' Shaped a generation of work on community, trust, and political participation.

nodes.label: Human Genome Project
nodes.description: International public consortium and Celera completed a high-quality human reference genome. Catalyzed the precipitous fall in sequencing cost that fueled modern genomics and personalized medicine.

nodes.label: Wikipedia
nodes.description: Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger launched a multilingual, openly editable encyclopedia. Within two decades the largest reference work humans have ever produced.

nodes.label: Mass social networking
nodes.description: Facebook (then Twitter, Instagram, TikTok) made the social graph an addressable platform. Reshaped political mobilization, journalism, advertising, and the texture of public discourse.

nodes.label: Isolation of graphene
nodes.description: Andre Geim and Konstantin Novoselov peeled single atomic layers off graphite with adhesive tape. Created a model two-dimensional material with extraordinary electrical and mechanical properties.

nodes.label: Optogenetics
nodes.description: Karl Deisseroth and colleagues used algal channelrhodopsin to make individual neurons light-controllable. Gave neuroscience a high-resolution causal probe of brain circuits.

nodes.label: Induced pluripotent stem cells
nodes.description: Shinya Yamanaka reprogrammed adult somatic cells into pluripotent stem cells with four transcription factors. Bypassed the ethics of embryonic cells and seeded regenerative medicine.

nodes.label: Cloud computing (AWS)
nodes.description: Amazon Web Services rented out elastic compute and storage as utilities, eliminating the data-center capex barrier. Enabled the modern startup economy and large-scale machine learning.

nodes.label: Smartphone (iPhone)
nodes.description: Apple's iPhone collapsed phone, browser, music player, camera, and a sensor-rich pocket computer into one slab of glass. Reshaped daily computing for billions within a decade.

nodes.label: Human microbiome characterization
nodes.description: Sequencing-based studies (HMP, MetaHIT) catalogued the trillions of microbes living on and in humans and their links to digestion, immunity, and neuropsychology. A young, fast-moving field.

nodes.label: Bitcoin and the blockchain
nodes.description: An anonymous paper described a decentralized digital currency anchored by proof-of-work consensus. Whether or not Bitcoin endures, the cryptographic ledger pattern reshaped distributed systems thinking.

nodes.label: ImageNet large-scale labeled dataset
nodes.description: Fei-Fei Li and collaborators assembled millions of human-labeled images across 1000 categories. The benchmark and training data that made deep convnets visibly successful in 2012.

nodes.label: Cancer checkpoint immunotherapy
nodes.description: Approval of ipilimumab and later anti-PD-1 antibodies released the immune brake on T cells, producing durable remissions in melanoma and other cancers. A new pillar of oncology alongside surgery, chemotherapy, and radiation.

nodes.label: Discovery of the Higgs boson
nodes.description: ATLAS and CMS at the LHC observed a particle consistent with the Higgs boson predicted in 1964. Closed the Standard Model's remaining major prediction.

nodes.label: LIGO detects gravitational waves
nodes.description: LIGO observed the merger of two black holes via tiny strain in laser interferometer arms. Opened gravitational-wave astronomy and confirmed a century-old prediction of general relativity.

nodes.label: AlphaGo defeats top Go players
nodes.description: DeepMind's AlphaGo beat Lee Sedol 4-1, then later AlphaZero learned superhuman play tabula rasa. Demonstrated deep reinforcement learning at the limits of strategic complexity.

nodes.label: Neural machine translation
nodes.description: Google and others replaced statistical phrase-based translation with end-to-end neural sequence models, dramatically improving quality. The first widely felt application of deep learning to language.

nodes.label: Reusable orbital rocket
nodes.description: SpaceX recovered Falcon 9 first stages, dramatically lowering launch costs. Catalyzed a private space-launch industry and large LEO constellations like Starlink.

nodes.label: Quantum advantage demonstration
nodes.description: Google's Sycamore performed a sampling task estimated infeasible on classical hardware. Contested in detail but a milestone toward useful quantum computation.

nodes.label: mRNA COVID-19 vaccines
nodes.description: Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna deployed mRNA vaccines against SARS-CoV-2 within a year of the genome's release. Validated mRNA as a fast, programmable vaccine platform.

nodes.label: AlphaFold 2 solves protein structure prediction
nodes.description: DeepMind's AlphaFold 2 predicted protein 3D structures from sequence at near-experimental accuracy on most CASP targets. Made structural biology a starting condition rather than a project of years.

nodes.label: Diffusion image generation
nodes.description: Stable Diffusion, DALL-E 2, and Midjourney made photorealistic image generation from text broadly available. A new mass-market content medium - and a new policy problem.

nodes.label: ChatGPT and consumer LLMs
nodes.description: OpenAI's ChatGPT made large language models a usable consumer product, with rapid adoption across knowledge work. Recentered AI from research curiosity to economic infrastructure.

nodes.label: Multimodal foundation models
nodes.description: GPT-4, Gemini, and Claude unified text, image, audio, and video understanding in single large models. The dominant AI architecture as of 2026 - and the substrate of most agentic and tool-using systems.

nodes.label: Agentic AI systems
nodes.description: Foundation-model agents that plan, use tools, write and run code, and operate over long horizons moved into production for software engineering, research, and customer support. An active and rapidly evolving frontier.

nodes.label: Logical-qubit quantum error correction
nodes.description: Demonstrations of logical qubits whose error rates are lower than their physical components, with surface and concatenated codes on superconducting and atomic platforms. The threshold milestone toward fault-tolerant quantum computing.

nodes.label: GLP-1 agonists for obesity
nodes.description: Semaglutide and tirzepatide, originally diabetes drugs, achieved 15-25% weight loss and large cardiovascular benefits. Reshaped the medical understanding and treatment of obesity within a few years.

nodes.label: Base and prime editing
nodes.description: David Liu's lab developed CRISPR derivatives that change individual DNA letters or rewrite short stretches without double-strand breaks. The next-generation toolkit of precise gene editing.

nodes.label: Paleogenomics and Neanderthal DNA
nodes.description: Svante Paabo's group sequenced Neanderthal and Denisovan genomes from ancient bone, revealing interbreeding with Homo sapiens. Made deep-time human evolution a sequencing problem.

nodes.label: Implantable brain-computer interfaces
nodes.description: Neuralink, Synchron, BrainGate, and others demonstrated humans controlling cursors, prosthetic limbs, and speech synthesizers via implanted electrode arrays. Early but moving from lab to clinic.

nodes.label: Unicode universal text encoding
nodes.description: Unicode set out to encode every script in a single code space. Made multilingual digital text routine and forced a long, careful confrontation with the world's diverse writing systems.

nodes.label: Fusion ignition at NIF
nodes.description: The National Ignition Facility achieved the first laboratory fusion reaction yielding more energy than the laser drive delivered to the target. A milestone toward inertial-confinement fusion.

nodes.label: Direct air capture at scale
nodes.description: Climeworks' Mammoth and other plants moved carbon dioxide direct-air-capture from kilotonne to plausibly multi-megatonne pathways, with falling per-tonne costs. A nascent climate-engineering industry.

nodes.label: Perelman proves the Poincare conjecture
nodes.description: Grigori Perelman proved the geometrization conjecture using Hamilton's Ricci flow, settling the Poincare conjecture. Refused both the Fields Medal and the Clay Millennium Prize.

nodes.label: Wiles proves Fermat's Last Theorem
nodes.description: Andrew Wiles proved Fermat's Last Theorem via the modularity of elliptic curves, closing a 358-year-old problem. A landmark for the Langlands program and modern arithmetic geometry.

nodes.label: Friedman and the natural-rate hypothesis
nodes.description: Milton Friedman argued that there is no long-run trade-off between inflation and unemployment, attacking simple Keynesian Phillips-curve policy. Reset macroeconomic policy after the stagflation of the 1970s.

nodes.label: Rawls' Theory of Justice
nodes.description: John Rawls grounded liberal political philosophy on principles parties would choose behind a 'veil of ignorance.' Revived analytic political philosophy as a serious enterprise.

nodes.label: Nash equilibrium
nodes.description: John Nash defined an equilibrium of mutually best responses for non-cooperative games. Generalized von Neumann's analysis and became the standard solution concept of game theory.

nodes.label: Wilson's sociobiology
nodes.description: E.O. Wilson's Sociobiology placed social behavior of all animals - humans included - within an evolutionary framework. Provoked enduring controversy and seeded evolutionary psychology and behavioral ecology.

nodes.label: Dawkins' Selfish Gene
nodes.description: Richard Dawkins popularized the gene-centered view of evolution and coined 'meme' for cultural replicators. Reshaped public understanding of evolution and influenced cultural-evolution research.

nodes.label: Csikszentmihalyi's flow
nodes.description: Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi formalized 'flow' as the optimal experience of being absorbed in challenging tasks. Helped seed positive psychology.

nodes.label: Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm
nodes.description: A famously elegant graph algorithm, soon followed by Dijkstra's structured-programming critique of GOTO. Set the tone for an academic discipline of algorithm design and software discipline.

nodes.label: Mass-market streaming video
nodes.description: Netflix streaming and YouTube's video-by-default web changed how billions consume entertainment, news, and education. Moved content delivery from broadcast to bit-rate-adapted unicast.

edges.description: Symbolic cognition is the substrate for symbolic visual representation.
Coordinated knowledge transmission was required for the cumulative learning of plant and animal husbandry.
Writing encodes spoken language; no language, no script.
Early pictographic writing emerged from a longer tradition of mark-making and visual abstraction.
Administrative record-keeping for agricultural surpluses was the proximate driver of writing in Sumer.
Durable written transmission was required to accumulate and systematize a body of geometric results.
Formal logic depends on a stable written notation for the propositions being analyzed.
Written case histories were the empirical backbone of the Hippocratic corpus.
Printing presupposes a writing system to mass-reproduce.
Greek geometric methods, transmitted via Arabic translation, were the substrate al-Khwarizmi systematized symbolically.
Greek methods of exhaustion (Archimedes via Euclid's tradition) prefigured the integral.
Newton presented mechanics in explicitly Euclidean geometric form.
Bacon explicitly positioned inductive empiricism against Aristotelian deduction; the new method was a successor.
The formal-logic tradition (Aristotle to Frege to Hilbert) framed the question Turing answered.
Cheap paper, diffused west through the Islamic world, was the physical substrate that made movable-type printing economically transformative.
Algebra over the place-value decimal system requires zero as a manipulable number.
Symbolic algebra was the language in which calculus was eventually written.
Al-Haytham's explicit hypothesis-experiment-conclusion structure prefigured the methodological program later codified by Bacon and Galileo.
Heliocentric astronomy (via Kepler's laws) was the empirical target Newtonian gravity had to recover.
Smith applied systematic empirical observation to social and economic phenomena.
Lavoisier's quantitative experimental chemistry is a paradigm case of the scientific method applied to matter.
Darwin's argument was an extended inductive case built from systematic observation over decades.
Controlled experimentation (Pasteur's swan-neck flasks, Koch's postulates) was the methodological core.
Newtonian mechanics is written in the language of calculus Newton himself invented.
Maxwell's equations are partial differential equations - calculus is their grammar.
Maxwell took Newtonian field-free mechanics as the foil against which to formulate the electromagnetic field.
General relativity is Newton's theory of gravity superseded; Newtonian gravity remains its low-curvature limit.
A rational chemistry with clean elemental identification was the necessary precursor to periodic classification.
Evolution by natural selection demanded a physical mechanism of inheritance; DNA structure provided it.
Special relativity emerged from Maxwell's equations' invariance, and special relativity is GR's flat-spacetime limit.
The ultraviolet catastrophe in classical EM blackbody radiation was the empirical anomaly that forced quantization.
Paleomagnetism - measuring fossil magnetic-field orientations in rocks - provided decisive evidence for seafloor spreading.
Periodicity was the empirical pattern that quantum theory eventually explained via electron shell structure.
Solid-state semiconductor physics is quantum mechanics applied to crystal lattices.
X-ray crystallography and quantum chemistry made structural determination of biological macromolecules possible.
Shannon's discrete, formal framing of communication grew alongside the formalization of computation.
Networked computing presupposes computing - Turing's universal machine model is the abstract base case.
Neural networks are programs; they need a substrate of universal computation.
Digital networking required the cheap, reliable, miniaturizable digital logic the transistor made possible.
GPUs - the proximate enabler of AlexNet - are densely packed transistor arrays.
Packet switching, error correction, and channel design all rest on Shannon's framework.
Information-theoretic concepts (entropy, mutual information, cross-entropy loss) sit at the core of modern ML.
Genome editing is sequence-specific manipulation of the DNA double helix; knowing the structure is prerequisite.
Internet-scale image datasets (ImageNet) were the empirical substrate that made deep learning effective.
Transformers superseded convolutional architectures as the dominant deep learning paradigm for sequence and vision.
Symbolic visual marking presupposes the symbolic-cognition substrate of language.
Pictographic writing inherited a deep tradition of representational image-making.
Both belong to a long African tradition of structured tally marking that prefigures arithmetic.
Tally records were a precursor to administrative writing systems.
Pre-literate tally and grouping practices fed into the earliest written arithmetic.
Both reflect the Holocene shift to cooperative human-animal partnerships.
Verbal coordination of breeding and training was needed for sustained selective pressure.
Settled food storage created sustained demand for ceramic vessels.
Kilns capable of firing pottery taught the high-temperature control required to smelt ore.
Bronze is the alloy of the copper smelters once they learned to add tin or arsenic.
Iron metallurgy succeeded bronze as smelters mastered higher temperatures and reducing atmospheres.
Communal monumental sites are entangled with - though they may slightly precede - settled farming.
Stored agricultural surplus was the resource fortifications were built to protect.
Crop agriculture in arid floodplains depended on canal networks.
Centralized water management generated the administrative complexity that drove writing.
Both belong to the lineage of carefully controlled material transformation in early China.
Wheeled construction logistics were part of the toolset that built Roman civil works.
Mesopotamian and Egyptian scribal traditions developed in close contact and traded ideas.
Semitic workers borrowed hieroglyph signs and assigned them consonant values acrophonically.
The Phoenician script is the direct descendant of the Proto-Sinaitic letters.
The Greeks adopted Phoenician letters and added explicit vowels.
Aramaic script descends from Phoenician and carried it across the Persian empire.
Greek scientific writing - including Euclid - depends on the alphabetic literacy the new script enabled.
Aristotle's analytic prose was made possible by the Greek alphabetic writing tradition.
Hippocratic case histories required a literate medical culture writing on portable surfaces.
The Aramaic-then-Arabic literate culture of the Near East carried mathematical learning across centuries.
The same Harappan urban culture produced both monumental drainage and a still-undeciphered script.
Seal script systematized characters that had evolved from Shang oracle-bone forms.
A unified script gave paper its empire-wide audience.
The Maya script descends from the broader Mesoamerican proto-writing tradition.
Long Count dates were inscribed in the Maya glyphic system.
Chomsky explicitly recognized Panini as a forerunner of generative formal grammar.
Saussure trained as a Sanskritist; Panini's analytical model shaped his structural linguistics.
Mesopotamian and Egyptian arithmetic traditions exchanged techniques across centuries.
Greek geometers absorbed Babylonian computational practice via trade and travel.
The Greek geometric tradition built on Babylonian and Egyptian computational practice.
Hipparchus' trigonometry took its sexagesimal angular framework from Babylonian astronomy.
Egyptian geometric problem-solving fed into Greek deductive geometry.
The Thales tradition of demonstrative geometry was the seed Euclid systematized.
Pythagorean theorems and number theory are core content of the Elements.
Plato's mathematical idealism is steeped in Pythagorean number-mysticism.
Archimedes wrote in the rigorous geometric idiom Euclid established.
Apollonius developed his conics within the deductive framework of the Elements.
Archimedes' method of exhaustion is the deepest pre-modern source for the integral.
Apollonius' theory of conics gave Kepler the mathematical objects to describe planetary orbits.
Zeno's paradoxes were a long-standing provocation that the calculus eventually resolved.
Ptolemy's mathematical astronomy is built on Hipparchus' chord tables and observations.
Arabic algebra absorbed Diophantine techniques alongside Hindu numerical methods.
Aryabhata's place-value astronomy was an immediate Indian precursor to Brahmagupta's formal treatment of zero.
Indian astronomical works reached the House of Wisdom and informed al-Khwarizmi.
Kerala-school power-series anticipated, and may have influenced via Jesuit transmission, European calculus.
Boolean algebra is a successor to syllogistic, treating logical relations as algebra.
Frege extended Boolean propositional logic with quantifiers into modern predicate logic.
Russell and Whitehead built the Principia Mathematica on Fregean logic.
Goedel's theorems explicitly take Principia Mathematica as their formal target.
Turing's computability work answered the same Hilbert-program question Goedel attacked.
Leibniz's dream of a 'characteristica universalis' helped seed the algebra-of-logic project.
Leibniz extended Pascal's adding machine and shared the dream of mechanical reasoning.
Babbage's machines stand in the lineage of mechanical calculators Pascal and Leibniz initiated.
Babbage adopted Jacquard's punched-card scheme for instruction control of the analytical engine.
Lovelace's algorithm was written for Babbage's analytical engine.
Shannon's master's thesis applied Boolean algebra to switching circuits, founding digital logic design.
Galen worked within - and against - the Hippocratic tradition.
The Edwin Smith papyrus belongs to the Egyptian medical tradition descending from Imhotep.
Hippocratic empirical case-history practice has Egyptian precedents.
Both belong to the same Indian Ayurvedic medical tradition; Charaka focuses on internal medicine and Sushruta on surgery.
Indian materia medica and clinical concepts entered Avicenna's synthesis through Arabic translation.
Chinese pharmacological knowledge reached the Islamic medical tradition along the Silk Road.
Avicenna's Canon incorporated and extended Dioscorides' Greek pharmacology.
Avicenna's Canon synthesized Galenic anatomy and physiology with Arabic medical practice.
Avicenna built on al-Razi's clinical writings and disease classifications.
Vesalius taught from the Canon before producing the anatomy that overturned Galenic detail.
Harvey's quantitative circulation argument required Vesalian anatomical accuracy.
Ibn al-Nafis described pulmonary circulation centuries before Harvey, with possible transmission via Renaissance translations.
Pare's humane wound treatment is part of the surgical lineage that anesthesia transformed.
Malpighi's microscopic discovery of capillaries closed Harvey's circulation theory.
Hooke's celebrated micrographs convinced others - including Leeuwenhoek - of the microscope's scientific value.
The microorganisms Leeuwenhoek revealed became the candidate agents of disease that Pasteur and Koch implicated.
Hooke's discovery of cellular structure two centuries earlier prefigured the cell theory.
Microscopic anatomy of plants and animals laid the empirical groundwork for the cell theory.
Mendel's particulate inheritance presupposes that heredity is carried in cells.
Cajal extended cell theory to the nervous system as discrete neurons.
Jenner's empirical success at preventing disease became part of the case for an infectious-agent theory.
Pasteur self-consciously extended Jenner's vaccination strategy to other diseases.
Attenuated vaccines were one of germ theory's earliest practical payoffs.
Eradication required understanding the smallpox virus as a specific transmissible agent.
Eradication scaled Jenner's two-century-old vaccination strategy worldwide.
Antisepsis (Lister) joined anesthesia to make modern surgery possible.
Semmelweis's hand-washing data was empirical evidence that something invisible transmitted disease.
Snow's cholera mapping showed an invisible waterborne agent before germ theory was accepted.
Both founded statistical-spatial public health within a few years of each other.
Antibiotics presuppose an understanding of bacteria as discrete pathogenic agents.
Mid-century antibiotic success raised public expectations and funding for new vaccines.
Polio vaccination is a 20th-century descendant of Jenner's preventive paradigm.
Modern mRNA vaccines extend the inoculation tradition Jenner founded.
mRNA vaccines depend on understanding DNA-to-RNA-to-protein flow at the molecular level.
GLP-1 agonists extend the insulin lineage of hormone-replacement endocrinology.
Both depend on - and contributed to - the modern understanding of how to manipulate the immune system.
Long, complex surgery is impossible without anesthesia.
CT reconstructs cross-sections from many X-ray projections.
Both inaugurated digital cross-sectional medical imaging.
GPS satellites must apply general-relativistic timing corrections to be accurate to meters.
Genome sequencing relies pervasively on PCR amplification of DNA.
DNA fingerprinting amplifies its target loci by PCR.
Knowing the human genome made it worth - and tractable - to edit.
Microbiome research adapted human-genome sequencing pipelines to communities of microbes.
Modern human reference genomes are the comparison set for ancient DNA.
Base and prime editing are next-generation derivatives of CRISPR-Cas9.
Modern gene therapy uses CRISPR-derived tools alongside the early viral-vector approach.
Al-Haytham worked within and against the Aristotelian optical tradition.
The Organon and Physics share a common method and metaphysical vocabulary.
Copernicus' De revolutionibus rebuilt and replaced the Ptolemaic system.
Copernicus knew of Aristarchus' ancient heliocentric proposal.
Kepler explicitly built on al-Haytham in his work on vision and optics.
Tycho responded to Copernicus by building observational programs to test planetary models.
Kepler distilled his three laws from Tycho's Mars observations.
Newtonian gravity had to recover Kepler's laws as derived consequences.
Galileo's telescopic phases of Venus were decisive evidence for heliocentrism.
Galileo's experimental practice gave Bacon's program empirical teeth.
Oresme's geometric kinematics pre-figured Galileo's analysis of free fall.
Newton wrote against the Cartesian vortex picture even as he absorbed its mechanistic premises.
Pendulum-clock precision gave Newtonian physics its empirical timepieces.
Young's interference experiment confirmed Huygens' wave theory of light over Newton's corpuscles.
Calculus is most naturally written in Cartesian coordinates.
Logarithms were the first mass-deployed numerical handling of continuous magnitudes by exponents.
Kepler used Napier's tables to crunch his planetary data.
Laplace's analytic probability builds directly on the Pascal-Fermat foundations.
19th-century descriptive statistics inherited the analytic probability of Laplace and Gauss.
Modern social choice and statistics descend from the analytic probability tradition.
Riemann was Gauss's student; his geometry generalized the differential geometry Gauss began.
Riemannian geometry generalized the non-Euclidean alternatives into manifolds with arbitrary metrics.
General relativity is mathematically a Riemannian theory of spacetime.
Both contributed to the late-19th-century shift toward abstract structural mathematics.
The logicist program presupposed Cantorian set theory as a foundation.
Maxwell used Fourier methods on his partial differential equations.
Wavefunctions are most naturally analyzed via Fourier decomposition.
Quantum mechanics inherits the Lagrangian-Hamiltonian variational framework.
GR's field equations follow from a variational principle in the Lagrangian tradition.
Faraday's experiments required steady electric current, which Volta first supplied.
Maxwell mathematized Faraday's intuitive picture of lines of force.
Electrochemistry quickly extended Lavoisier's quantitative chemistry into ionic decomposition.
Helmholtz formalized energy conservation in the framework Carnot's heat-engine analysis suggested.
Clausius extended Carnot's cycle into the modern second law and the entropy concept.
Statistical mechanics, which Boltzmann derived from Clausius' framework, set up the blackbody problem that Planck solved.
Energy conservation - now mass-energy - underpins relativity's accounting.
Special relativity resolved the Michelson-Morley null result by abandoning the aether.
The invariance of Maxwell's equations was a direct motivation for special relativity.
General relativity is special relativity made local in curved spacetime.
Einstein extended Planck's quantization from oscillators to light itself.
Bohr's quantized orbits assumed photons of definite energy emitted in transitions.
Bohr's model added quantized orbits to Rutherford's nuclear atom.
Wave mechanics replaced Bohr's quasi-classical orbits with full quantum amplitudes.
The Dirac equation is the relativistic completion of non-relativistic quantum mechanics.
Pauling derived chemical bonding from quantum mechanics applied to electrons.
Pauling's bonding rules were the toolkit Watson and Crick used to reason about DNA geometry.
Lemaitre's expanding universe is a solution to Einstein's field equations.
Hubble's redshift law gave Lemaitre's expanding universe direct empirical traction.
Cosmic expansion implies a hot dense early state and its leftover radiation.
The CMB is the predicted relic radiation of Lemaitre's primeval atom model.
Both belong to the early-20th-century discovery of phenomena that classical astronomy could not explain.
Inflation explains otherwise puzzling features of the CMB - flatness, horizon, and structure-seed spectra.
LIGO confirmed a century-old GR prediction by directly detecting spacetime ripples.
The Higgs sector is part of the quantum field theory that grew from quantum mechanics.
The Standard Model is the successful merger of special relativity with quantum mechanics.
Mid-century nuclear physics built the institutional and instrumental tradition that the LHC continues.
Dalton's atoms gave Lavoisier's elements a microphysical interpretation.
Avogadro's law presumes Daltonian atoms combining into molecules.
